mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive mean during rate hike periods with a t-statistic greater than 2.0, or the rank correlation between rate changes and returns falls below -0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal triggers but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances, or the out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45% during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth that drives corporate earnings revisions upward, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to generate a positive mean 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index increase above 0.5% during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above 0.5% during tightening periods produces negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the predictive alpha vanishes when the federal funds rate slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index growth erodes real corporate earnings and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL values correlate positively with 20-day forward returns with a rank IC exceeding 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or inflation spikes fail to predict negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls increase exceeding 200,000 jobs confirms robust economic momentum that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payrolls above 200,000 trigger negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the signal predictive power decays to a rank IC below 0.02 during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable consumer price index levels indicates a soft landing environment that maximizes equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of high GDP growth and low inflation fails to generate positive 20-day returns, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates paired with rising unemployment signal a policy-induced recession risk that triggers rapid equity deleveraging, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Elevated rates and rising unemployment coexist with positive 5-day forward returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect reverses direction during slowdown regimes.
